SPACEjoint: clinical analysis & feature selection pipeline for SPACE project

by Venla
28.05.2025

SPACEjoint: clinical analysis & feature selection pipeline for SPACE project

by Venla
28.05.2025

Overview of SPACEjoint

Develop a reproducible R pipeline to evaluate the relevance, redundancy, and optimal representation of features derived from clinical data, t-CycIF, and molecular profiles. 
The pipeline will support robust feature selection + guide downstream analyses + rank the most clinical-relevant features.
Currently focusing on: 
Clinical analysis using features with prior knowledge. 
Perform feature selection and importance analysis to identify informative and non-redundant variables across datasets. 
Future goal is to: 
Construct a patient-level feature matrix incorporating key clinical, spatial, and molecular variables.
Apply archetype analysis to visualize patient positioning in the feature space; identify biological or clinical archetypes and their dominant features.

Feature selection

Boxplots to visualize the data
Correlation matrices (Pearson, Spearman) for manual correlation check

Feature selection

Boxplots to visualize the data
Correlation matrices (Pearson, Spearman) for manual correlation check

Feature selection pipeline

Cramer’s V for categorical variables
Functions for VIF, LASSO and Random Forest for automatized feature selection
Features chosen in two or more of these were chosen 
Recursive elimination with repeated CV using C-index for these selected features

MOFA (multi-omics factor analysis)

Indentifies latent factors that explain variance across different data types
MOFA works best when 10+ variables per dataset
